OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

The Company’s failure to submit certain regulatory reporting documents across financial years 2022, 2023 and 2024, as outlined in the VFA Rulebook, within the stipulated deadline. MEASURES On 10 July 2026, the MFSA agreed to settle pending matters with the Company after demonstration of goodwill by the Company. By means of a settlement agreement entered into between the Company and the MFSA, the Authority imposed an administrative penalty of forty thousand five hundred and sixty Euro (€40,560) on Everest Network Ltd. PUBLICATION This notice is being published in terms of the Authority’s Settlement Policy – https://www.mfsa.mt/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/MFSA-Settlement-Policy.pdf.

Business Activity: VFA Service Provider
FINDINGS:
The MFSA’s investigation found failings in respect of:
Article 28(1)(c) of the Virtual Financial Assets Act and Rule R3-2.3.4.4.2 (iii) of the Virtual Financial Assets Rulebook Chapter 3 – Virtual Financial Assets Rules for VFA Service Provider (the “VFA Rulebook”), for failing to observe the requirement to obtain the necessary approvals from the Authority prior to undertaking changes to its shareholding structure amongst its existing shareholders; and
The Company’s failure to submit certain regulatory reporting documents across financial years 2022, 2023 and 2024, as outlined in the VFA Rulebook, within the stipulated deadline.

Under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), the difference between being Authorized and Notified by the Malta Financial Services Authority (MFSA) dictates how a firm can provide cross-border Reception and Transmission of Orders (RTO) for crypto-assets on behalf of clients:

Authorized (CASP): Standalone crypto firms must obtain a full Crypto-Asset Service Provider (CASP) License. To offer RTO cross-border in the EU/EEA, authorized CASPs must formally notify the MFSA of their target host states. The MFSA then passports the service.

Notified (Exempt Entities): Traditional financial institutions (e.g., credit institutions, investment firms, MiFID II entities) do not need a new, separate CASP license. Instead, they rely on their existing licenses and are only required to notify the MFSA at least 40 days prior to offering cross-border crypto RTO

62515731
The more I look, I actually think it’s a good thing lol. Thank you schizo for sharing this and giving me heart attack for 2 hours until I got home from wage slave to research myself

The change from "Authorized" to "Notified" means your firm’s regulatory burden shifts from undergoing a rigorous, standalone Crypto-Asset Service Provider (CASP) licensing process to leveraging your existing traditional financial licenses (e.g., MiFID II) with a simpler advance-notice requirement.Transitioning to "Notified" (as an exempt entity) significantly changes your cross-border RTO framework:Licensing Requirement: You no longer need to obtain a specialized CASP license from the Malta Financial Services Authority (MFSA). Your traditional financial institution license suffices.Passporting Process: Your RTO service is not "passported" in the traditional sense; instead, you rely on a prior notification to the MFSA instead of seeking a new approval for host states.Time-to-Market: Instead of waiting for the full CASP licensing and formal passporting clearance, you only need to submit an advance notice 40 days before commencing cross-border RTO services in target EU/EEA host states.Ongoing Supervision: You remain supervised primarily under your original regulatory framework (e.g., MiFID II) rather than being subject to standalone CASP compliance rulebooks, while ensuring adherence to MiCA's conduct-of-business rules for crypto.

62515775
It’s a good thing you stupid Indian Ranjeet

Under MiCA, a Maltese authorized CASP (Crypto-Asset Service Provider) switching to a "notified" (cross-border) status means they are no longer setting up physical branches abroad but are instead exercising their "European Passport" to provide reception and transmission of orders (R&T) remotely to other EU/EEA Member States from Malta.
